channel.title: /biz/ Active (filtered, with replies)
channel.description: Filtered out: general, gme, bbbyq. OP + first 3 replies + last 30 replies.

item.title: What’s stopping you from buying BTC? — 138 replies — 315.8/hr
item.content:encoded:
What’s stopping you from buying BTC?

Open thread • Replies: 138 • Updated: 2026-05-29 20:50 UTC

OP

What is it? Fear? Lack of conviction? Waiting for lower prices? Or do you genuinely think Bitcoin fails long term?

Curious what’s holding people back at this stage.

Early replies (first 3)

62271721
>>62271712
Btc needs a catalyst. It probably will be yield curve control with a new name. It could also be codifying the SBR into law which is less likely. Or my personal favorite schizo theory, it's discovered that Iran was mining and selling bitcoin to fund it's endeavors and that their supply is finally exhausted.

62271922
>>62271712
Gays in it.

62271933
>>62271712
everybody and their femboy lovers above two-digits IQ are waiting for BTC to dump below at least 40k by July. Why anybody buy now? 4 years cycles never lie

Latest replies (last 30, oldest → newest)

62283769
>>62276880
ARMA hasn't passed yet, but there's already a Reserve.

62283901
THE BIG DUMPING
>every cycle 90-70% loss. why would it be this time different.

62283926
>>62283901
diminishing returns mean diminishing drawbacks, retard

62283940
>>62282847
I hope that big brain stuck around, for my sake

62283952
>>62271712
Bitcoin is a pyramid scheme and a waste of electricity. I was an early adopter of bitcoin fully thinking it was a scam and I sold mine in 2016 after I had enough to retire. I understand the whole point about fiat currency but what I think we need to do is just get rid of money all together and use assets as money like stocks and commodities. Money was invented so people didn't have to carry their assets around but know we all walk around with computers in our pocket that would allow us to trade our assets like how things used to be. I should be able to go to McDonalds and buy my food with corn for example. Bitcoin only exists for countries to bypass sanctions and if you buy bitcoin you are just giving more money to Putin and all the other scammers and terrorists.

62285517
>>62272225
They've got their preferences

62285578
>>62283375
Yeah, DeFi hacks are getting pretty crazy lately. That’s why multiple audits, formal verification, penetration testing, and cryptography reviews matter much more now with this much capital involved.

62285585
>>62272647
You'll regret your choices lol

62285589
>>62272667
You think you're smart?

62286335
>>62271712
No money, no job
They literally won't even take me at minimum wage places because I don't speak spanish.
So, no way to generate the income to buy BTC in the first place.

62287162
>>62274011
Work for it anon

62287177
>>62283952
You can already by McDonalds with corn. Use credit card and pay off credit card with corn futures. The problem is your dumbass is bleeding wealth by keeping your net worth in corn (or companies, or anything besides bitcoin) because people are going to make more corn (and ever faster each year due to tech advances) and inflate away your wealth.

62287383
>>62285578
but why remains the hack

62287392
>>62286335
If you're married at don't have money, you deserve a divorce

62287393
>>62271712
It's a gamble and I only bet on sure things. In fact I like to have my profits guaranteed under contract and insured BEFORE I spend a dime of my own money. I always sell BEFORE I buy so I know exactly how much I'm making in advance.

62287464
Most of the retards have been bled dry. BTC only exists now as a system for whales to lead exit pumps and bleed the occasional new entrants.

62287628
>>62287383
Most of the biggest DeFi disasters over the years involved bridges, custodians, or avoidable trust assumptions somewhere in the stack. That's exactly why security architecture gets attention now.

62287630
>>62280441
You're thinking the wrong way

62287632
>>62282426
Well, depends on them

62287634
BTC has just tracked the nasdaq for years until recently and now it just sucks ass no matter what happens.

Hype has peaked. Most of it is owned by boomers now and its not a hedge against anything it just dumps the hardest on bad news until boomers, banks and third worlders get tired of bagholding and it goes sub 20k forever

62287690
>>62287634
So you're saying btc is only a big hell hype?

62288099
>>62287634
nonsense

62288112
>>62273994
So it's not worth it?

62288281
>>62271712
Indifference. I got into the market when the BTC was $3K. but I bought Link instead. Pleased with my decision. I respect what it has done and continues to do with the world but I get excited for smart contracts more, which the BTC doesn't have.

62288653
>>62271712
Because I got caught with my pants down avgcost 95k, and I've been trimming it and using that money as play dump money. So far the 15k I sold from Btc and put into Sandisk have turned in 110k already, and the 10k into gold are at 14k, and it's an actual asset. Never buying that retarded shit again. I still have like 30k and maybe I'll keep that, but chances are I'll probably sell and ride the SpaceX pump and dumo with it. Put in 50k into it, sell 50k when it doubles in price, keep the rest as gains. Did the same shit with Micron, Sandisk and silver, take profits and just keep the rest. This is a hype based market and btc has no hype anymore.

62290061
>>62288112
We'll find out when TBVs actually launch.
Then you can judge the product based on what it does, not on what random people online tell you to think.

62290076
>>62275451
Ok, just buy altcoins

62290078
>>62272002
ahahahahahahahah

62290085
>>62275501
If BTC goes to zero, alts are probably even more cooked.

62290480
>>62287464
If it's such an obvious scam, why are you still here watching it?

item.title: The Market Turbo Shits, But I Rise Like Jeet Flies — 35 replies — 140.0/hr
item.content:encoded:
The Market Turbo Shits, But I Rise Like Jeet Flies

Open thread • Replies: 35 • Updated: 2026-05-29 21:12 UTC

OP

BAT bros? You still there? What the fuck is going on? LINK shits. BTC dumps deadly diarrhea. Orange man shitposts. But this piece of shit is +15%? Is Lobes flipping the switch? Hold me. Also. Jennie lewds please

Early replies (first 3)

62285628
no one still owns BAT
... right?

62285632
>>62285628
Apparently I do. Jennie doesn't though

62285713
YOU KNOW THE DEAL.

TP - WHERE ARE MY THOTS AT? IT'S BEEN A WHILE.

Latest replies (last 30, oldest → newest)

62286053
>>62286043
Wow. That's some deep pasta bro. Missed this one

62286063
>>62285713
>IT'S BEEN A WHILE
It's been a hot minute. Sup, lad?

>>62285966
>Where are you?
o/
>do you still own BAT?
Of course, lad *sobs *

Haven't seen a thread in a minute. Was planning on doing some thot bait threads when we get a new JanJan.

62286087
>>62286063
>Blue hair
Gross

62286122
>>62286087
I mean if you want me to post men or trannies for you, just give me the word ^_^
_

Anyone else getting some pretty gnarly memory leaks from Brave recently?

62286148
>>62286122
This is better. Thanks. Looks like a boobier version of that Claire Manson fashion bitch on instagram. Thanks TP

62286153
>>62286063
>>62286122
Hawt
Got any uber thicc goths??

62286226
BAT is total shite. You have to be retarded to still believe in this garbage

62286297
>>62285619
if i had invested in LINK instead of BAT i'd be a millionaire right now, and probably retired

62286350
>>62286122
no fucking trannies please.

just hot ass bitches, keep em coming TP, and thanks legend

62286359
>>62286297
If I ate one pile of dogshit instead of another pile of dogshit I'd be puking my brains out harder right now too

62286450
>>62286148
NP. Feel free to drop a request.

>>62286153
Of course!

>>62286297
I absolutely abhor LINK, but same u_u. I literally should've put more money anywhere else.

>>62286350
I'm just fucking around.
>Legend
Much appreciated!

>>62286359
At least STINK pumped. All I wanted was $4 BAT.

62286624
>>62286450
fuark that bitch can get it

62286763
>>62286450
Would happily suffocate devouring her ass while she sits on my face 10/10 slut

62287337
https://www.instagram.com/veronicanakama/

62287362
>>62286450
moar

>>62286297
same, I don't like to think about it.

62289019
>2026
>BAT
wew

62289108
>>62286063
>>62286122
jolly good jiggling jugs TP
>>62286450
seconding moar of this btggf or even her username

62289174
How is this board still discussing dead shitcoins from 10 years ago
Does no one here like making money

62289182
>>62287337
lmao she do be living high off the BAT scam

62289202
>>62289174
BAT threads are just places for us to lament and to talk to the friends we met along the way

62289359
>>62286122
what the fuck is this whore's name

62289403
>>62286122
>gnarly memory leaks
no its working damn fine on my machine
I had to fix youtube by element blocking the share links though

62289404
>>62286122
Man

62289650
>>62287337
The fuck you doing? We were supposed to keep this from the peasants

62290320
>>62287337
you mean her name isn't even jennie? wtf scammed

62290344
>>62289108
Hey Lad! Long time, no see. I'll switch PCs in a bit and see if I can find the profile for you.

>>62289359
Give me a bit and I'll see what I can do for you. I have like 8 more from that set and some other ones in the meantime I can post.

>>62289403
I'm getting like 5GB+ memory usage from a single YouTube tab. Tends to only happen on livestreams or really long videos. Let me know if you have a request!

>>62290122
Are you requesting feet?

62290355
>>62287337
That's so foul, blowing up her page like that. She better private that shit ASAP.

>>62287362
I'm working on it, but here's another thicc goth for (You).

62290540
>>62290344
>I'm getting like 5GB+ memory usage from a single YouTube tab]
its a known bug yt's end, the like and share (etc) interface below the video is caught in an endless live loop
to fix it do this: go to settings then find shields then select content filtering then scroll down to create custom filters and paste this in the box field www.youtube.com###menu
then save changes
let me know how you get on

62290561
>>62290344
>I'll switch PCs in a bit and see if I can find the profile for you.
thanks TP, you're the best of 'em
>Hey Lad! Long time, no see
I'm working abroad again
trying to get settled in
what about you? doing well I hope

item.title: Kek XLM is absolutely ripping and is up multiple places overtaking monero and link while stinkies are at $8.99 LMAO HAHA… — 27 replies — 108.0/hr
item.content:encoded:
Kek XLM is absolutely ripping and is up multiple places overtaking monero and link while stinkies are at $8.99 LMAO HAHA…

Open thread • Replies: 27 • Updated: 2026-05-29 21:12 UTC

OP

Kek XLM is absolutely ripping and is up multiple places overtaking monero and link while stinkies are at $8.99

LMAO HAHAHAHAHAHA OH NO NO LINKIES OH NO NO

Early replies (first 3)

62287876
>>62287844
we aren't cuckolds, okay? We just like knowing that Sergey keeps getting richer while Chainlink is stuck at 2020 prices

62288485
kek stinkies

62288499
>>62287844
xlm always does this before the bull..

Latest replies (last 30, oldest → newest)

62288539
>>62287844
this image is kinda hot...

62288741
>>62288539
Are you sure?

62288748
>>62287844
Well XLM pumps 40% and on same even bigger news LINK dumps 9%

Other asset is suppressed and another isnt

62288856
>>62288748
>Other asset is suppressed and another isnt

KABOOOOM! So bullish

62288910
>>62288539
It makes no sense. The SF is paying chainlink to be a part of SCALE. Theyre paying for this integration with the DTCC.

62288931
>>62288910
>chainlink

62288957
>>62288910
Remember when department of commerce announcement happened and wormhole pumped 20% while LINK dumped 5%

Its basically repeat of that, demoralization & suppression

Big wallets keep accumulating and stacklets keep selling

62289062
>>62288957
That just means the pumps are meaningless and based on nothing, just pure speculation by the people controlling the token.

62289072
>>62288957
>Big wallets keep accumulating

62289486
>>62288910
>cryptos are paying for the integration with DTCC
>my special crypto does not tho, that's legit
???

62289511
>>62289072
Yeah all those metrics are fake goy, do not buy LINK

62289523
>>62289511
>teh metrics
what metrics bruh?

62289559
>>62287844
link's time is coming

62289566
>>62289511
>SAAAAAAAAAR BIG WALLETS KEEP BUYING SAAAAR WHALES ARE ACCUMULATING

62289658
DTCC keep repeating over and over that they're going to use all kinds of chains. Stellar is just one of them.
Meanwhile Chainlink has a quasi monopoly.

yet this is how the market reacts.
No wonder crypto is getting is ass beat by the stock market so badly.

62289736
>>62289658
>saaar the market wrong saaar chainlink very valuable, note it

62289745
>>62289658
claiming that "they're going to use all kinds of chains" inherently implies that they're going to use the corresponding blockchain tokens as well, since you know, that's required when interacting with each chain
having said that, please point to me one instance of DTCC stating that they're using the LINK token

62289756
>>62289745
>please point to me one instance of DTCC stating that they're using the LINK token
The Chainlink products they're using need the Link token.

62289763
>>62289756
>can't even bring up a single instance

62289813
>>62289763
Instance of what?

62289925
>>62287844
I don't care about your (((compliant))) coin

62290228
>>62287844
I started the link cuckold meme. KNEEL

62290247
>>62289745
Chainlink will literally abstract away any gas token

One token all chains (the one and only LINK)

62290559
>>62287844
MOM IT'S DOING IT AGAIN

item.title: Day 13 of cyber-snailposting every day until ICP is at $1000 — 25 replies — 100.0/hr
item.content:encoded:
Day 13 of cyber-snailposting every day until ICP is at $1000

Open thread • Replies: 25 • Updated: 2026-05-29 21:02 UTC

OP

Early replies (first 3)

62284484
>>62283370
.

62284489
Please fuck my ass

62284919
>>62284489
you say that every day, creep.

Latest replies (last 30, oldest → newest)

62284922
>>62283370
ICP is finished OP and I say that as a cucked ICP holder. All their technical people are leaving and the price chart will forever look like the euthanasia roller coaster until the price hits 3 cents. They had an opportunity to become THE AI chain and totally fumbled the opportunity with mismanagement. I have no faith in Dominic's strategic direction at this point. I'm dissolving my 6month neuron with the hopes to sell by EOY to write off a loss for tax purposes.

62284936
/pol/ brainlets btfo, ok? Moonman scam finally getting reveal

62284942
>>62284922
ICP has a partnership with the government of Pakistan, Switzerland and UN. Also has a partnership with HBAR, Maestro and Elon Musk’s brother is one of the holders.
ICP has one of the fastest growing developers and more thanks to Caffeine AI.
Some shit coins like kaspa only have 5 active devs while Dfinity has 200 + and thousands of people building every day.

The price in 2021 was manipulated, you are a cocksuckers.

62284948
>>62284922
>ICP is finished OP

* you are a cocksucker

62284992
>>62284942
>ICP has a partnership with the government of Pakistan

62284996
>>62284922
When you sell because of disinformation, just don’t kill yourself when ICP does a 100x.

62285005
>>62284992
80 million people is a joke to you? At least it’s being utilized by someone unlike 99% of snakeoil out there like Solana that don’t do anything besides memes.

62285370
>>62284992
.

62285887
>>62284992
.

62285944
>>62284942
>thousands of people building every day
This is just a plain lie. Nobody wants to build with gay Motoko. Andreas Rossberg left Dfinity 4 years ago now, and CTO Jan left last year. It's over for ICP's development. Single-line commits on github don't count worth shit.
>>62284996
Selling before it goes to $0.03

62285947
>>62284922
none of that matters for the price to go up

62285963
>>62285944
The chart doesn’t lie. Not only are people building on ICP but Caffeine made it possible for thousands of projects to be stored on the internet computer web service.

eat shit

62285965
>>62285944
cope

62285982
>>62285963
>>62285965
TOPKEK
so desperate for any attention

62285988
>>62285963
Caffeine is a grift scam by Lomesh the scammer and Dom, after they realized their multi-million dollar throwaway project HotOrNot (now YRAL) is a failure.

62286401
>>62285988
You realize that you can make video games on caffeine? How in fuck is that a scam? I fucking used it. You people really got nothing better to do than spread disinformation

62286421
>>62286401
Calm down sperg, caffeine is expensive and not a practical solution for anyone. That's why it hasn't made any impact CBR in anyways since it became publicly available. Cope and seethe all you want, but ICP is over. Dominic Williams is not a good person

62287613
>>62286421
Piss off. There is no crypto that can do what ICP does. 99% of altcouns can’t even store a phone photo. You’re just a hater who probably lost money.

62289113
>>62287613
>You’re just a hater who probably lost money.
There isn't a single ICP investor who hasn't lost a shitload of money to pay for this man's all-expense-paid luxury lifestyle in Switzerland, complete with a Porsche 911.

62289837
>>62284484
.

62289975
>>62289113

TOPKEK

>so true

62290531
>>62283370
icp 50 usd soon?

item.title: Saar! Buy my Hyderabad. Saar, DO NOT Redeem! Bloody Bastard Bitch! — 17 replies — 68.0/hr
item.content:encoded:
Saar! Buy my Hyderabad. Saar, DO NOT Redeem! Bloody Bastard Bitch!

Open thread • Replies: 17 • Updated: 2026-05-29 21:08 UTC

OP

Saar! Buy my Hyderabad. Saar, DO NOT Redeem!
Bloody Bastard Bitch!

Early replies (first 3)

62290396
>>62290392
Kek
Will fade soon

62290399
revenue only go up, no competitors sir very good token.

62290406
>>62290399
>no competitors
>edgex appears behind you

Latest replies (last 30, oldest → newest)

62290415
>>62290406
sir do not fud please. many moons ahead

62290421
>>62290399
>muh revenue

This shit will have heaviest baggiest in crypto soon

62290426
>>62290392
60b fdv
22% circulating

62290428
>>62290392
This, so much this. Only jeets hold the best-performing crypto of 2025 and 2026.
True aryans hold russian scams like Chainlink and Ethereum.

62290432
>>62290392
>>62290396
>>62290399
>>62290406
>>62290415
>>62290421
>>62290426

62290458
>>62290432
past performance is not indicative of future results

62290466
>>62290432
mentally ill tranny, my post had nothing to do with link

62290474
>>62290466
No, you're somehow even more pathetic chasing a bucket shop with washed metrics as a purely cope play because you missed out on the most tokenholder-aligned project since BTC.

62290484
>>62290474
banks will use edgex

62290506
>>62290432
Nobody gives a flying fuck about your shitcoin saar, pls go and buy the space ipo with the good goyim!

62290530
>>62290506
If you don't care then why coordinate this FUD spam anytime Hype is mentioned and not any of the other goyraped alts regularly posted here? I've never hard shilled Hype here once because I legitimately don't give a fuck about this place anymore

62290534
>>62290530
I am saaring in every saar shitcoin not just this one.

62290542
>>62290534
Fair, I'll stop talking about my bags and just join in on the link FUD

62290547
Saaaarses lets not waste more izzat and settle this in the traditional way. We all go to the ganges tomorrow and the one who drinks more holy water will win.

item.title: Honestly owning rentals made me realize how helpless alot of adults are now. — 19 replies — 52.3/hr
item.content:encoded:
Honestly owning rentals made me realize how helpless alot of adults are now.

Open thread • Replies: 19 • Updated: 2026-05-29 20:55 UTC

OP

Honestly owning rentals made me realize how helpless alot of adults are now.

I bought a bunch of duplexes down south back around 2018-2020 because the numbers made sense. Cheap property taxes, cheap insurance, cheap properties in general. One duplex I paid $124k for is bringing in around $1925/mo total now and the tennants renewed instantly after the last increase.

Had a tennant last week who submitted a maintenence request because the microwave “stopped heating.” Property manager drove over there and the door wasnt shut all the way.

Another guy called because his screen door came off the track. PM slid it back in place in about 10 seconds and the guy goes “much obliged sir” like he just repaired farm equipment from the 1800s or something.

Whats weird is the older tennants almost never bother anybody. Pay on time. Maybe ask for a sink fix every few years. Younger ones send paragraphs over tiny stuff.

Not even trying to sound mean but I geniunely think people are less capable now than they were 20 years ago.

People online act like owning rentals is some impossible genius level thing but honestly half the job is just dealing with grown adults who dont know why the wifi stopped working after they unplugged the router to move furniture around.

Early replies (first 3)

62283853
>>62283816
It never sounds worth the money

62283860
How much of your revenue do you devote to hookers

62283871
>>62283816
I will buy one of these when I make it from Link. Probably in a college town. I won’t care about any profits but negotiate BJ’s for rent if they are unable to pay

Latest replies (last 30, oldest → newest)

62283878
>>62283816

Like this post, what exactly do you contribute?

62283893
>paying a property manager when you're only making 2k a month
i got sick of landlording. Every single time a tentant text me I was filled with dread and could never be bothered doing anything about the problems. You pay a shit load of taxes on it here too, not worth

62283964
>>62283816
>Live in a disposable consumerist society
>shocked when people don't know how to fix shit
Only reason I learned to fix shit was because I was too poor to replace. Now that I'm doing better, I still fix my shit because my money came from blood sweat and tears and I'll be damned if it goes to some fucking company so a CEO can take in more profit and his kids can go to Europe on a whim

62284183
>>62283816
so what you're telling me is owning a rental is like running daycare
yeah that sounds shit

62284244
>>62283878
It´s a subtle demotivation thread.

62284260
>>62283853
>>62284183
Yeah I feel like it should only ever be something temporary.
If you've got the money and brains to be a landlord you've got the money and brains to run a small convention center or camping ground at much less stress cause you can operate on predicable cycles.
I think the only thing more retarded than being a landlord long-term is deciding to run a cinema.

62284369
>>62283878
the property

62284379
>>62284369
lol

62284669
>>62283816
I rent a house and rarely ask for help/repairs because my landlord is a nice old widow, but to be honest if I didn't like my landlord I'd do it a lot more often, even on little things.

I do make it a point to not invest too much time or money in upkeep of the house, since I don't own it. That's throwing money away. Landlords owe it to tenants (and themselves!) to keep the place nice. So if a tenant wants some little thing fixed, even if it's not actually broken, it really should be the landlord's duty to fix it. It's one of the perks of renting, honestly.

62284712
>>62283816
Also, a $124K duplex earning $1925/mo - so that will take like ~6 years (probably closer to 7-10 years considering the costs of fixing things up, hiring property mgmt, taxes, possible vacancies, etc.) to pay off before you're earning pure profit.

That actually doesn't sound too bad as long as you have a real job bringing in real income the whole time

62284771
>>62283816
I lived at this one building and pipes were getting clogged every month. It was a horrible experience for everyone, tenants, maintenance guys and the pm. Multiple other issues like AC breaking down or electrical issues. It was cheap but i had to leave.

62284898
>>62283816
As long as you keep the place clean and safe I really don't give a fuck what you do. The problem is most landlords don't keep their properties clean and safe.

62285996
>>62283853
standard rental property will make money but sellers want to sell their properies at a 4-5% cap rate and its not really worth buying one unless you get a 10%+ cap rate. youd be better off buying into a REIT if you want real estate exposure

62286915
>>62284669
if its not actually broken, then it doesnt need to be fixed. at that point, you are making discretionary decisions about a place you dont own.

62290485
>>62284712
considering how retarded the housing market has been you can probably sell it for double that if you get tired of the monthly income
>>62283816
while the microwave thing was retarded you generally don't want to touch anything in rentals. its not my house so why would i take responsibility when it can be done by the official maintenance guy and anything going wrong will be his problem then

62290497
When I first moved to an apartment in 2014, maybe within a year, a lightbulb went out and I literally called my super to change it. He didn't even complain, he just came, did it, and left. (In my defense, it was a lightbulb in the ceiling, but I've also been very un-handy)

item.title: I have a feeling ETH will rise back — 13 replies — 44.7/hr
item.content:encoded:
I have a feeling ETH will rise back

Open thread • Replies: 13 • Updated: 2026-05-29 20:59 UTC

OP

Early replies (first 3)

62286999
Standard chartered says ETH to hit $40k

62287001
>>62286999
4.8 trillion market cap? Really nigga?

62287010
>>62286995
From $2.5k down to $1.5k over and over again and you’re still broke. How?

Latest replies (last 30, oldest → newest)

62287015
>>62286999
>>62287001
since bitcoin is "digital gold" I assume they just looked up the market cap of silver to make this prediction

62287019
>>62287001
>Distributed computation and storage with active developments in homomorphic encryption is not worth 4.8 trillions dollars.
Why are there so many retards here?

62287186
>>62286995
cope

62288056
>>62286995
I told you to sell in august. Not that i did sell all but a lot.

62288098
>>62286999
Standard Chartered just made up a bunch of numbers and said "this is our forecast" lol

Also checked

62288128
>>62287019
>homomorphic encryption
sounds gay as fuck, no wonder your shitcoin is trading at the same price as 2021

62288202
>>62288128
It's closed source computation.
It's current value is reflective of the ignorance of the network's capabilities.
Can you explain how the Ethereum network works? Save your fingers. You've already demonstrated that you can't.

62290487
>>62286999
I fucking wish it even hits 10K by then, I would retire from the passive income.

62290501
waiting to get back in. 500 seems a fair entry!!!!

62290515
>>62290501
n-no! it will never fall that low!!! you will buy it for $5000 from me and I will be laughing at you!

item.title: Chinese tech stocks — 17 replies — 11.6/hr
item.content:encoded:
Chinese tech stocks

Open thread • Replies: 17 • Updated: 2026-05-29 19:49 UTC

OP

These last couple of years has been pretty generational for American tech stocks. Anything addressing the AI rollout has pretty much gone vertical. Mag7 is at ath, MU, SNDK, etc. all up 10-20x in a year. And there is plenty more. This is on the back of massive private and public AI spending (7.8tril by 2028 or whatever). These were great opportunities and could very well continue to go up.

I've started looking at other countries. KOSPI up almost 200% in a year, NIKKEI almost 70% in a year. However I have noticed Chinas market is pretty much flat/slightly down. I know China is dealing with various issues (stagflation, unemployment, low productivity, gov intervention etc.), but this feels like a massive discrepancy.

I'm gathering a list of some solid Chinese tech stocks as I think they have the most room to grow. In particular I'm liking BABA, BAIDU, CQQQ and XIOAMI. These companies are all exposed to AI growth, cloud, compute and data centre rollout across China and the world. If these companies were in the USA they would automatically be trading close to 10x they are now just cause thats the norm.

I am also looking at Vietnamese tech stocks too but thats more niche. I think the US market continues to do well and thats fine, but the bulk of the money has been made. Chinese tech stocks is my prediction and I would love some input as to why these aren't a good buy.

Early replies (first 3)

62286501
>>62286463
China doesn’t want people investing into their market. Simple as that.
They don’t want funny money shit.
China values stability. You can invest into their blue chips I guess.

62286558
>>62286501
Stability will mean China loses the AI race and enriches their biggest rival. I understand it may have been that way, but at some point they need to compete. The companies are solid, revenue and infrastructure. It almost feels like at some point the blue chips I mentioned will need to pump to compete on the stage with USA.

62286564
>>62286463
If I said the American stock market was a casino, a joke, a farce or a circus, you would know exactly what I mean. (Even if it isn’t 100% true obviously)
The Chinese and their culture don’t want that for their stock maket.

Latest replies (last 30, oldest → newest)

62286572
>>62286558
>loses the AI race
there is no race. the AI mania in the west is equivalent to china spending billions on empty uninhabited ghost cities. crony capitalism

62286595
>>62286564
I value your input, I just feel like at some point the difference of 300b for BABA and 3t for AMZN will eventually even itself out, and the Chinese counterparts stand to gain the most in this situation. Just an observation.

>>62286572
AI has revenue to back it up. And China will always need their own versions of tech so they don't overly rely on the west. Sure its crony capitalism but the $$ being transferred between different companies is ridiculous (AI -> hardware -> memory -> AI) and surely China has to engage or be left victims to the empire of the USA.

62286600
>>62286558
>>62286572
Yeah. There isn’t a “race.”
China also has cheap energy apparently.
Also, the fact Bassent and Trump literally go on tv everyday and say
>China is far behind the usa
means China probably isn’t behind or far behind. Even Jensen Huang said “China isn’t behind” and then immediately had to correct what he said.
Since China has cheap emergy, they can just use more chips or whatever. Jensen did a segment on this.

62286604
>>62286595
> surely China has to engage or be left victims to the empire of the USA.
Go watch some Jensen Huang interviews. I am sure he knows more than us. He doesn’t say China is behind.

62286660
>>62286604
I will thank you. But in a way does that not also mean these companies are undervalued? pic rel seems like they are a combination of google and amazon. Or maybe I am just looking at it through the lens of Americanisation.

62286903
>>62286600
>then immediately had to correct what he said
To the gulag with the infidel!

62287114
>>62286660
Idk, the companies are sound for sure but chinese government fuck with companies or do these things. I lost like $6000 on didi. Lmao.
>>62286903
It really isn’t a race but yeah watch his interviews. He explains it better.

62287265
>>62286463

62288363
Bump

62289048
>>62286463
Alibaba is most undervalued. Did you know that they manufacture their own chips? T-Head semiconductors belongs to Alibaba Group.

62289095
>>62286463
Chinese regulators have way less tolerance for market concentration and monopoly/oligopoly profits.

62289180
>>62286660
Corporate valuation is supposed to reflect the present value of all expected future profits. Revenue does not automatically mean profits.

The Chinese government has little tolerance for market concentration and the associated monopoly rents, so, if a private Chinese company manages to get into a position to extract monopoly rents, this is unlikely to be sustained. In a perfectly competitive market, profits tend toward zero.

62289193
>>62286558
Chinese companies do not depend on public capital markets to raise money to the extend US companies do.

"Losing" a "race" assumes there's a finish line and winner-takes-all in an irreversible manner. However, this is not the case, because there is no real moat in AI. The only moat is the artificial moat caused by the export controls on microchip manufacturing equipment, which is a moat the Chinese are going to bridge, so it is not so much a moat as it is speed bump.

62290173
>>62286463
CXMT is about to get IPO in the next couple months raising 29.5 billion yuan with 10% total shares, so a marketcap of 295 bn yuan, and it's projected to make 100-200 bn yuan this year. The rule for Chinese opening auction is that you cant make an offer more than 9 times the IPO price, so we are looking at a maximum initial marketcap of 29.5*10*9=2655 bn yuan.

Assuming it hits the limit, it would put its forward PE between 13 and 26. IMO it's a very good buy and Im currently looking into how/if I can buy it. Keep in mind all of it's current revenue comes from DDR4/5 alone, HBM is where the money is at and they are about to mass produce it (HBM3 so it's one gen behind micron/sk hynix)

BABA is very solid, I see qwen regularly on AI rankings

UH OH WAGIES

Open thread • Replies: 33 • Updated: 2026-05-29 20:51 UTC

62287004

62287005

62287007

62287014
>>62287005
>NOOOOOO DON'T MAKE FUN OF MY HECKIN ROCKET! NOOOOOOOOOO

62287024
Manay ominous signs this past week

62287030
>>62286974

62287042
>>62286974
Musk must be laughing his ass off

62287046
>>62287007
Either this or emergency unpaid overtime shifts.

62287049
KWAB

62287052
CLEAN UP THE DEBRIS, WAGIE!!!

62287125
>>62286974
bezos can suck the shit of my asshole

62287128
>>62286974
So? Eill stonks go down and crypto up now? No? So?

62287260
>>62286974
AMAZON x XRP PARTNERSHIP?????????

BOOOOOOOOOOOOOOOOOOOOOOOOOOOOOOOOOOOOOOOOOOOOOOOOOOOOOOOOOOOOOOOOOOOOOOOOOOM!

62287271
>>62287052
what an ugly freak god damn nigga even with 270 billion dollars being this ugly has got to be rough.

62287278
Gender studies be one of them classes where you realize society really out here making random rules then acting like them rules dropped from the sky . The whole subject talks about how people get treated different based on gender, race, class, and identity. It ain’t just about “men versus women.” Nah, it’s deeper than that. It’s about power, who got it, and who keeps the system running like a broken carnival ride .

Back in the day, society pushed these roles heavy. Dudes supposed to be tough, work jobs, never cry. Girls supposed to stay pretty, quiet, cook food, and smile through suffering like customer service employees on minimum wage . Gender studies basically walks into the room and says, “Yo, who invented these goofy rules?” That’s where people start talking about gender as a social construct. Meaning society made a lot of these expectations up over time and everybody just downloaded the update without reading the terms and conditions .

Another big thing in gender studies is intersectionality. Big word, simple meaning. Different struggles stack together like bad Wi-Fi connections. Somebody can face problems because of gender, race, money, or all three at once. The system don’t hit everybody equally. Some people start life on easy mode while others spawn into expert difficulty with no tutorial .

Media also catches strays in this class because movies, ads, music, and social media keep pushing stereotypes 24/7. Folks grow up seeing the same roles repeated until they think it’s “normal.” Gender studies tries to challenge those dominant narratives and make people question why society moves the way it does.

At the end of the day, the class teaches that identity, power, and culture all connected. Also teaches students how capitalism be profiting off stereotypes while everybody else just trying to survive and pay rent .

62287284
NO BATHROOM BREAKS TOMORROW

12 HOUR SHIFTS SAT AND SUN

62287347
>>62287260
so bullihs at this..

62287354
>>62287271
He literally looks like any other MF in the street

62287575
>>62286974
Cool firework show Jeffy
>didn't even leave the ground
did they forget to undo the bolts?

62287805
So now that crypto is officially dead and buried, where will all the crypto money flood into?

62287806
>>62286974
RIP BRAVE GIRLS

62287807
>>62287805
ai, after that biotech, after that real estate (2035), after that pm (2040)

62287808
>>62286974
>"spacex will be overvalued!!!"
spacex made things look easy, they have an incredible moat

62287893
>>62287284
>IF YOU WAGIES ARE EVEN A MINUTE LATE --

62290016
>>62287807

62290301
>>62286974
Back to work wagies

62290337
>rocket an hero
>podium holding it still intact

Yeah the twin towers sure fell because of jet fuel

62290338
>>62286974
It's wild that we're nearing a CENTURY since the first V-2 rocket travelled into space and these retards STILL can't get it right. Just unimaginable levels of incompetence.

62290349
>>62286974
why is there this stupid space race? there aint shit up there

62290482
>>62290349
It's all a huge scam. None of them care about or actually intend to go to space. What they're doing is creating the framework to sell a dream to investors. By the time everyone realizes it's never going to happen, the people running the scam will have cashed out and moved onto the next scam.

item.title: how do you avoid your infos being leaked ans the subsequent home jacking? — 35 replies — 54.0/hr
item.content:encoded:
how do you avoid your infos being leaked ans the subsequent home jacking?

Open thread • Replies: 35 • Updated: 2026-05-29 20:38 UTC

OP

France has seen an explosion in crypto kidnapping. This explosion came after the state demanded citizens to declare their "self hosted" wallets and their balances. There also was a data leak from some kind of private investment company after a hack.
People with balances as low as 2 btc have been targetted and successfully forced to give up all their assets under threats. Some people living abroad have had their relatives kidnapped.

How do you avoid this shit? all the regulations on crypto have been an absolute disaster, the only end goal is to steal your wealth via taxation and state control. This totally ruins the whole crypto thing. There is no point in holding large amounts of cryptos if it puts a target on your back and on your familly and you can't sleep well at night.
Now many anons might think this is a french thing but eventually more leaks will happen and nobody will be safe. there will be lists with your name and address and all the purchase you did in the last years, even maybe your depo address that criminals will be able to track and see what most likely happened with your balance.

One simple mitigation measure would be to use many exchanges, many adresses, as to make it appear you hold less, but this is also an annoying process that require strict management and doesn't guarantee additional leaks at the state level if they group all your trading infos.
You should stop using any KYC, stop paying taxes on government that don't care at all about your safety, and go full down the crypto ecosystem giving up off ramps as much as you can.

Early replies (first 3)

62287742
Generic India hate thread disguised as something else.

62287753
>>62287731
>France
That's what you get for living in Africa.

62287759
>>62287753
while i can't disagree, the problem remains. if you think living in the usa or germany makes you safe from this issue you're delulu. Usa is known for its violent crime just wait til a list of easy 1 Mil++ robberies on individual citizens start going around and see what happens

Latest replies (last 30, oldest → newest)

62287835
>>62287731
Dont live in 3rd world countries like france and obviously most kidnappings happen due to money flex not leaks

62287837
the leftists do this. The other half doesn't but get jewed constantly.
anyway that's a stupid argument

62287850
>>62287753
France voted for it, they deserve all they get and more.

62287859
>>62287850
>>62287821
>>62287831

While your answers are completely selfish and retarded, i took a visit to rubic and rubic actually allows cross chain swaps between bitcoin and many others stables so that's one way you can do crypto to crypto trade without KYC, and sell high to rebuy lower without every retard gov agency being aware of it.
I'm there are a lot of solutions out there.

62287875
>>62287731
the gov home address leaks are the insane part of this coz you cant really prevent that without not reporting (which is kinda impossible if you ever want to spend a decent amount)

only way to prevent i guess is having a holiday home and just pointing to that

62287892
>>62287821
Database leaks happen all the time. Its just a matter of time before a KYC exchange gets breached and your info gets leaked

62287918
>>62287731
>vidrel
Poor wagie, it sucks that everything is filmed now. He can't forget this even if I bet he wants to.

62287930
>>62287731
>This explosion came after the state demanded citizens to declare their "self hosted" wallets and their balances
All by design

62287931
>>62287731
>I have a lot of money in crypto/cash/gold
>Omg why am I a target for robbery?!
What did you expect, faggot? Life like this. It's the way it is

62287985
>>62287731
1. lie
2. do not ever disclose your balance
3. have 2 homes in case

62288016
>>62287931
very forward thinking anon.
Difference is with a bank account it's much more anonymous and you can't just get robbed in a wrench key attack.

62288126
>>62287892
>KYC exchange
retard. THE FUCKING STATE IS MAKING YOU DO IT

62288135
>>62287985
>GOY ID
Kek has spoken.

62288136
>>62287759
>easy robberies
Nigga I will kill anyone who comes around trying to rob me. I am armed and insane.

62288150
>>62288136
Yeah, imagine giving up your seed phrase to some angry African because it has a gun that it barely knows how to use.

62288560
>>62287731
that video brought me so much joy, thank you.

62289395
>>62288560
i couldnt imagine a stupider gimmick than a rollerblade setup in a high-end, indoor restaurant, where alot of soup, stew and curry-style foodstuffs are served.

It makes sense in an outdoors setting like in america, where you're serving lightweight packaged food, and its probably more efficient overall to use rollerblades. But this is pajeet-tarded.

62289432
>>62289395
Rollerblade service should only be done by red headed tomboys with short hair and freckles with a small chest, a bubble butt, and wide hips.

62289484
>>62288150
>>62288136
how about they kidnap your elderly parents or any other important relative and start cutting their fingers live if you don't give the seed? which is their new modus operandi and they did it in france to a guy who is living in dubai but his father lives here

62289530
>>62287731
1. Dont live in france
1. Dont live in europe
2. Have multiple dummy wallet and load it with small amounts of stables and some shitcoins
3. Dont live in france

62289586
>>62289432
Too bad here is your skinny gay jeet. Fuck you.

t. Jew that doesn’t deal in the real world

62289590
>>62287742
do not capitalize the word "india"

62289690
>>62289586
>t. Jew
we smell it. choke on a baby dick.

62289723
>>62287731
Lmao.

62289923
>>62289432
>>62289590
based takes

>>62289484
sounds like in that particular case, the guy was realllllly retarded and publicly shitposted his wealth on social media. If he lives in dubai, he doesn't need to declare shit as income retard.

62289932
>>62289923
he was just running a crypto related business in dubai. I'm telling you it's a matter of time before this happens more or less everywhere where death penatly isn't the norm for this shit

62289939
>>62289923
you think it's still a matter of bragging, we are well past that point. Criminals gangs have lists of people and their assets either from hacks, or government officials selling those infos. they hire street level retards thugs to do the job from another country. Somehow the criminals found out he was the ceo of some business in dubai, probably not very hard to find.

62289944
>>62289932
so then it had nothing to do with Franceistan's laws, and it was just a case of an exposed person getting exploited.

Police need to do alot more to curb organised crime, but Anarcho-tyranny is the order of the day in the west, commies are just plain evil.

62290407
>>62287731
>own firearms
>shoot home invaders
gee that wasn't so hard now was it?

item.title: Is HYPE overvalued? — 21 replies — 32.0/hr
item.content:encoded:
Is HYPE overvalued?

Open thread • Replies: 21 • Updated: 2026-05-29 20:37 UTC

OP

Is HYPE overvalued?

Early replies (first 3)

62289589
>>62289577
No, governance tokens that give you exchange fee discounts are, uh, a revolutionary breakthrough

62289600
>>62289577
>>62289589
Check how the last twitter hype token ended, SUI. Its down 80% and dead. HYPE will end the same.

62289622
It's value is too high relative to the rest of the market, but it's a bit undervalued at the moment for what it should be.

Latest replies (last 30, oldest → newest)

62289657
>>62289600
I mean the buybacks are bullish as hell. This shit jumped today because of SpaceX speculation. Will see similar scenes before Anthropic and OpenAI. Stay poor Bobo. Biz can be handed one of the best projects in a long ass time and still try to slide it.

62289688
>>62289589
Governance token that gets 99% of the revenue bought back and burned. That's 10 figures a year. That IS revolutionary coming from this shithole with guys like Hoskinsin and Richard Heart fleecing you goycattle for their blackhawks

62289695
>>62289577
Yes and no.
95% of perp traders have a negative pnl. They are only profitable because retards think they'll get rich quick instead of just buying spot
Eventually this fad will fade. HYPE only has a future as long as normies can be convinced to dump their paycheque into perps and lose it

62289708
>>62289695
>buy hype as long as you think people will keep gambling

here, I phrased it better.

62289718
>>62289695
Human nature will fade? Lol. Bold bet.

62289726
People still don't even know hyperliquid is a blockchain and hype is the gas token. There are already more dapps than in fucking cardano.

You're still early

62289730
>>62289657
Its a new paradigm

We will all be rich

62289744
>>62289688
checked, don't forget fat sirgay. he literally raped me back in 2018, and I'm continue being raped to this day. I demand raperations

62289758
Hype is going to get regulated out of existence and "they" will create a perps chain that is suitable for goys.

62289762
>>62289744
Uh oh stinky

62289791
on one hand, it has very little moat. many perps dexes have already come and gone, most with tokens that had some kind of revenue share. on the other hand, it has persisted against tough pressure from binance without folding, and its revenue is among the strongest across defi protocols.

62289800
>>62289577
Already a Unicorn bro

62289842
>>62289589
yeah thats what i thought and then BNB pumped to 100B+. Im not missing this one, faggot.

62289859
>>62289762
RAPE
>>62289842
surely it must go down again to 50 at least, right? right???

62289928
>>62289859
no

62290297
>>62289577
what is this FUD all of a sudden? is somebody jelouse?

62290356
>>62289577
No, but soon Aero will be the new Hype.

62290405
>>62290356
Nah, Nerva will be the new one. It can ONLY be mined via CPU and it's anonymous like crypto was supposed to be. Still a small MC so it has tons of growth potential. I haven't found any others like it yet, as anything similar has already had their growth spurt, but would love if anyone knows of some to diversify!

item.title: Just finished my first year of law school and I did really well, so I'm basically a lawyer now. — 57 replies — 26.5/hr
item.content:encoded:
Just finished my first year of law school and I did really well, so I'm basically a lawyer now.

Open thread • Replies: 57 • Updated: 2026-05-29 19:07 UTC

OP

Just finished my first year of law school and I did really well, so I'm basically a lawyer now. AMA law related /biz/ questions you may have.

Early replies (first 3)

62272819
>>62272812
Can i peepee in the poopoo or will i go to jail?

62272833
>>62272819
You may

62272835
>>62272812
Is it legal for a president to collab with a news outlet to pump & dump the stock market while insiders make bank on it?

Latest replies (last 30, oldest → newest)

62275816
>>62275176
Ass for most of my life but as I’ve gotten older, tits
>>62275435
I honestly don’t know yet, it’s only been one year and I’m not even dead set on which area of the law I want to practice in.
>>62275484
>What was your LSAT score?
170
>What is your school’s rank?
Mid 20s
>What was your GPA?
In undergrad or 1L? In undergrad it was a 3.4 in an easy major, I just didn’t give a shit in college. In 1L it was a 3.5
>Where are you interning this summer?
I’m RAing with a professor and doing a one month internship with a local judge later in the summer.

>How big is your pp?
5.8 inches massive

62275842
Do you ever consider yourself a second class profession compared to finance chads who value and trade and make money off the silly little financial obligation contracts lawyers write up?

62275952
>>62275842
Again dude I just finished my first out of three years of law school, so I don’t know what the actual practice looks like at all.

62276043
>>62275952
I'm just ribbing you buddy, a little white collar banter if you will

62277022
>>62275816
>170 LSAT

Acceptable.

>Mid 20s Law School

Acceptable.

>3.5 1L GPA

Acceptable.

>I’m RAing with a professor and doing a one month internship with a local judge later in the summer.

It’s over. You are cooked. BIG oof. Thanks for playing.

62277039
>>62277022
Why is it over? And why didn’t you address my penis size?

62277080
>>62277039
If you are not interning at an AMLAW 50 firm your 1L summer then you literally need to kill yourself.

[spoiler]My posts are intended to satirize the rampant one-upsmanship in the legal profession. I don’t actually care about your genitalia you dumb retard. It is a metaphor.[/spoiler]

62278084
>>62277080
Well don’t scare me like that anon. You freaked me out since some of the people in my class do have Big Law internships and it’s kinda tough not to feel jealous sometimes with how much they’re getting paid.

62278202
>>62278084
Funposting aside, (You) should be (mildly) freaked out because spending your 1L summer split between volunteering for a professor and a one (1) month stint with a (non-federal) jjudge is concerning. Especially if the stats (You) quoted me earlier are true.

(You) are underachieving.

62278255
>>62278202
I don't know what to tell you. I applied for a billion 1L summer positions and even got a lot of interviews but none of them panned out. It would have been nice to get something more prestigious looking but I'm just glad I finally got something lined up. i have heard also that as long as you do do something legal related 1L summer then firms don't really care what it was, is that not true?

I'm not actually aiming for Big Law or anything, I'm mostly interested in environmental and real estate law and potentially being a prosecutor. I dunno I've only taken the basic classes and it feels weird this expectation to know for certain what you want to do before you've learned anything about the field.

62278315
>>62278255
I appreciate your sincerity, and I will stop frogposting.

Forget about prestige. Focus on what will get you paid. I encourage you to network with local boutique and midsize firms. These days, Big Law internships are reserved for black lesbians (unironically). You’re doing fine.

Godspeed.

62278366
>>62278315
>Forget about prestige. Focus on what will get you paid
I am getting paid for the RA job though.
>I encourage you to network with local boutique and midsize firms
This might be self-evident as a 4chan poster but I absolutely hate networking, really suck at it, and would like to avoid it as much as possible. Is it really that essential to getting a good job? Do I just have to force myself to do it? I heard the 2L summer hiring process was supposed to be way chiller and less insane.

And thanks for your advice by the way!

62278395
>>62278366
Networking is somewhat required for any career path unfortunately. My condolences anon

62280144
>>62278395

62280365
>>62278366
You will miss out of some of the best opportunities in this profession if you don’t network, unfortunately. I only got my current job because one of my professors was willing to refer me to someone on the inside.

62280758
Are there any good legal loopholes for evading debt collection? I'm writing a story, and there's a chapter where the MC scrambles to hide his assets from the chapter 7 trustee.

62281712
>>62280758
I know several but will tell you none

62282639
>>62280758
>I'm writing a story
I dunno if I believe you there anon

62286326
>>62272812
Truebit was hacked in January you think a lawsuit has a chance?

62286469
>>62272812
How much are jews over-represented in law?

Can I get in with a science degree and mediocre GPA (3.5)? If i can't get a science job with my MS then thinking of pivoting into patent law.

62286482
what are the reputations of people who go into different types of law? like are torts people regarded as ambulance chasers? people who go into real estate considered retards? etc

62286539
how do I sue companies for discrimination during the interview process?

62286591
>>62273041
>the irish people have done nothing for humanity, and neither will you. You are niggers compared to Russians

62286708
>>62273090
What were your classmates like? Like how nursing is single moms and programming is white sexual deviants and engineering students are just a bunch of Aryan chads.

62286727
>>62272812
Kys lawfag

62286755
>>62286326
Probably not
>>62286469
Definitely overrepresented but I dunno how much.
>>62286482
I definitely know personal injury lawyers are seen as being kind of skeavy, the civil rights and public interest stereotype is they're very wealthy and privileged with their parents paying for everything but still act condescending towards everyone trying to get paid. I don't really know stereotypes for the rest.
>>62286539
You basically can't unless you have a smoking gun like emails between each other saying they were discriminating or they accidentally sent you a voicemail joking about that guy they just discriminated against
>>62286708
Probably 60% women, and 95% white other than one asian girl. There were a few different cliques but the only really shared trait was alcoholism and being nerds. Some are the friendly type of nerds and some the smug type of nerds. Like there was one guy who would go to TA office hours and when someone would ask the TA a question he would very quickly butt in and try to answer the question for the TA, then at the end ask the TA "is that basically right?" pissed people off. Most people were just normal though.
>>62286727
Why come?

62287528
I know you said it's only for /biz/ questions, but I'm really curious: do you think Jigsaw could ever be prosecuted? This is something we're regularly discussing on >>>/tv/.

62287539
>>62272812
Is gooning illegal if she doesn't notice?

62289826
>>62287528
The guy who kidnaps people and puts them in death traps? What would even be the argument for him not being guilty of murder? To be guilty of murder and found to be the cause of someone’s death, you have to be the but-for cause of their death (but for the defendant doing X, person would not have died) and the proximate cause (basically means them dying was foreseeable from defendants actions). But-for Jigsaw putting them in the traps, they would not have died and their deaths were extremely foreseeable
>>62287539
Yes it’s illegal, but you can’t be charged with anything you’re the only one who knows about

62290013
>>62287539
bit of gooning on the side dont hurt anybody

item.title: *continues to predict the price of BTC in your path* All you had to do was buy on green and sell on red, at least we're… — 57 replies — 23.6/hr
item.content:encoded:
*continues to predict the price of BTC in your path* All you had to do was buy on green and sell on red, at least we're…

Open thread • Replies: 57 • Updated: 2026-05-29 18:51 UTC

OP

*continues to predict the price of BTC in your path*

All you had to do was buy on green and sell on red, at least we're close to a buy signal now

prev thread:
>>62261213

Early replies (first 3)

62284885
How do I use this?

62284969
>>62284885
green = buy dips
red = sell rips
yellow = volatility likely, derisk
the site has a realtime FX-based confirmation layer - positive confirms buys and invalidates sells, negative confirms sells and invalidates buys
today FX is solidly green so early buys are defensible
chart gets updated every month with a new 4-week, next will be on Sunday

62284994
imagine buying bitcoin over 70K

Latest replies (last 30, oldest → newest)

62285480
>>62285468
Why look at the moon when you can look at the wave anon

62285577
how do i find this? website?

62285614
>>62285577
Watermark

62285615
>>62285426
>I just added leverage as an option to the trade simulator and it's clearly not handling leveraged losses right, lol. In fact I just realized it's probably counting a 1% loss at 10x as 1% and not 10x (or a 1% move as 1% loss and not a .1% move). I'll fix that
yeah, most likely. Definitely tune it, it's super useful to understand how tight should the strategy be.

explain me something, i'm seeing two future signals (may 29 and may 30), this won't change if i understand correctly, that means the may 30 buy signal will show up in the simulation you have on the website? but the confirmation layer will only be updated in that day, yes? I suppose the confirmation layer is the strategy gauge?
Finally when are the trades done? Immediately when a new daily candle starts? Or is there any rule?

i'm going to give you a follow for sure

62285636
>>62285615
>that means the may 30 buy signal will show up in the simulation you have on the website?
First one that confirms. If FX continues this positive tomorrow, 2h after US open it'll confirm. Honestly I think buys today are good but that's not what the forecast back on the 3rd said
> I suppose the confirmation layer is the strategy gauge?
The FX gauges are the confirmation layer, Strategy is an oscillator using several weighted factors (physics predictions, FX on different timeframes, seasonality, etc) that's supposed to give you a simple, accurate bias for the day. It's all WIP but in its defense it was screaming Strong Sell on the 25th and man was that accurate
>Finally when are the trades done?
They execute immediately on confirmation, right now I'm working on a fully automated bot that just uses Strategy and a basic Bollinger strat for entries/exits
>i'm going to give you a follow for sure
thanks anon! Likes and comments on the posts are maybe the biggest possible help right now

62285706
>>62285636
is there anything i can read about the schizo psychics this is based on?

62285733
>>62285706
From the other thread, Econophysics as a concept has been around since Bacherie's "Theorie de la speculation" in 1900, but what I'm doing is based on "Price variations in a stock market with many agents" from 1997 as a foundation, and "Financial brownian particle in the layered order-book fluid and fluctuation-dissipation relations" from 2014, and then applying a pretty simple schizo concept to take the reasoning one step further. The end result is predictive weeks in advance

62285770
>>62285733
Gonna look into it
tks anon

62285791
>>62285480
The moon creates the tides

62285803
>>62285791
>wild shrimp need tides to reproduce
>moon creates tides
>moon makes shrimp sandwiches possible
Thanks moon

62285830
>>62285103
>https://shiyu-coder.github.io/Kronos-demo/
this shit looks overfit nigga id backtest it at least

62285839
I have a similar model coded up in pine it is quite useless lol

62285878
>>62285839
lmao this one works a little better, my main problem was underestimating the violence we got on fairly faint signals this last 3 days, which might be partially the nature of the simulation - outputs start to get a little blurry 4-5 weeks out and then just turn into noise, which is why I do the updates every 4 weeks
Still, calling every major pivot this month using schizo math is pretty decent

62285897
>>62285878
shut up bot

62285919
>>62285897
no u

62285940
>>62285919
you probably work for the cia retard

62285967
>>62285940
>CIA
>giving away the free money cheat code to internet autists
doesn't seem very likely anon

62286025
>>62285967
I mean as long as this doesn't go full premium (as a ploy to ensnare gullible /biz/ tards) it seems like a good resource. Some ppl on this board want to stay poor or just FUD for fun. Pathetic behavior honestly.

62286076
>>62286025
It's nowhere close to where I'd feel comfortable charging people money for it. Trade bots/API feeds will probably be subscription, when that's done, but those are more for pro traders
There's also daily updates on the twitter account

62286347
>>62285878
>outputs start to get a little blurry 4-5 weeks out and then just turn into noise, which is why I do the updates every 4 weeks
Why not update weekly? or even daily?

62286447
>>62286347
>Why not update weekly? or even daily?
Basically, because it's a lot easier - there are a lot of inputs into the model that each have to be harvested and processed by me before they get fed into the sim. It runs into the hours each time. I can also cope that the current date's "conditions" are obviously the starting point of the forecast, so having them flattening out a little bit is easier to align
I do weekly updates on the site and the twitter account, but that's 90% just applying current price momentum and FX momentum to the existing physics-based forecast, which I have to get better at. I'm a nerd, not a trader

62286532
>>62284814
>buy may 17
>sell for a loss on May 25

thanks

62286552
>>62286532
>buy may 17
>short may 25
you're welcome

62286639
>>62286447
>because it's a lot easier
Fair enough. Although I'm sure at least some of that data gathering and processing could be automated.
>I'm a nerd, not a trader
So you don't use it all? I get that some people are very risk averse but if you're confident in your technology I don't see why you wouldn't at least dip your toes and make what is essentially free money.

I think I might do a bit of paper trading and I'll let you know how it pans out by the end of June.

62286700
>>62286639
>Fair enough. Although I'm sure at least some of that data gathering and processing could be automated.
A lot of it is! I keep getting tempted to outsource some of it to an LLM but some gut instinct says "you'd be really really dumb to do that," even a local one
>So you don't use it all? I get that some people are very risk averse but if you're confident in your technology I don't see why you wouldn't at least dip your toes and make what is essentially free money.
Oh, I use it, have been trading perps with it for years, getting more profitable as the model evolves. After the addition of the FX layer last spring, I can get away with some truly disgusting leverage. I just meant I don't have a background in trading or technical analysis, so I've been learning all of those skills on the go. It's all related but still a different skillset
>I think I might do a bit of paper trading and I'll let you know how it pans out by the end of June.
Please do, I'll post in here when there's updates but you can always get in touch via Twitter

62287275
>>62284814
I’m just gonna wait for the mega dump to flush the longs down to 60k..

62289468
>>62284814
L, and let me be perfectly clear, FG

62289671
>>62289468
So up from here? Don’t speak zoomzoom

62289838
>>62289671
This is where price should turn up - hybrid FX+physics says Buy because FX is so positive, physics officially flips green tomorrow, it all seems pretty good for bulls over the next 24 hours.

62289954
>>62287275
this. I just go all in bottom of the bear market and do nothing a couple years
